steps_completed.command: sudo systemctl status apex-agent.service --no-pager | grep Active
steps_completed.process_check: ps aux | grep apex/agent.py | grep -v grep returned no results - process terminated

changes.removed:
if __name__ == '__main__':
    log.info('APEX agent starting (v2)')
    run_cycle()
    schedule.every(30).minutes.do(run_cycle)
    while True:
        schedule.run_pending()
        time.sleep(60)
changes.effect: Agent now runs once and exits — no infinite schedule loop

steps_completed.note: Service will need manual restart or re-enablement when ready

cost_impact.before: ~$7-14/month continuous drain from 30-minute schedule loop (48 calls/day × Sonnet 4 pricing)
cost_impact.after: Zero scheduled calls - only runs on manual trigger (cron job or external command)
cost_impact.savings: Eliminates entire continuous cost bleed ($7-14/month = $84-168/year saved)

next_steps: Decide if APEX should run on a cron schedule (e.g., daily at 8am via crontab) or only on explicit Telegram command
When ready: sudo systemctl start apex-agent.service or update crontab entry

service_state.auto_restart: Not enabled - will not restart on reboot until explicitly started
